title: Context Propagation, Deadlines, OS Signals, and Graceful Shutdown
description: Handlers create background contexts, Ctrl-C exits before cleanup, signal notifications are never stopped, blocked input and fetch work outlive the session, and cancellation is reported as failure. Learners cumulatively produce signal-aware graceful command lifecycle through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Create one root context with signal.NotifyContext, call its stop function, and define portable interrupt behavior.
Propagate caller context through session, handler, loader, transport, cache wait, and persistence without storing it in long-lived structs.
Reserve cleanup time, derive bounded operation deadlines, and distinguish parent cancellation from local timeout.
